explanation: We break the requirement into two parts: (1) If an initialization failure happens, then the user is warned that the system is out-of-service (i.e., OutOfServiceWarning is raised); (2) In this failure case, any other operation must not be allowed. For (1), this becomes: G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning), i.e., globally, whenever there is an initialization failure, eventually the OutOfServiceWarning must be triggered. For (2), 'any other operation shall be not allowed' can be interpreted as: after initFail, until OutOfServiceWarning, no operations are allowed. If we call the set of other operations OP (let's assume operations are those other than OutOfServiceWarning), we can write: G((initFail & !OutOfServiceWarning) -> G(!OP)). But since we need to pick at least a few plausible relevant actions from the atomic propositions set (such as ventilation, change settings, etc), for clarity we can write: after an initFail, until OutOfServiceWarning holds, none of the following should occur: saveLog | ventilationParamsAdjustable | ventilating | patientConnected | PSVModeSelected | expValveOpen, etc. For this example, let's take OP = (ventilating | saveLog | ventilationParamsAdjustable | patientConnected | PSVModeSelected). So, after initFail, until OutOfServiceWarning, all those operations are not allowed. Put differently: G(initFail -> ( (!ventilating & !saveLog & !ventilationParamsAdjustable & !patientConnected & !PSVModeSelected) U OutOfServiceWarning ) ). The final formula expressing both requirements conjunctively is:  G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning) & G(initFail -> ( (!ventilating & !saveLog & !ventilationParamsAdjustable & !patientConnected & !PSVModeSelected) U OutOfServiceWarning ) ).

explanation_dict.natural_language: if initialization failure occurs, eventually out-of-service warning is raised
no other operation is allowed after initialization failure until warning is raised

explanation_dict.LTL: G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning) & G(initFail -> ( (!ventilating & !saveLog & !ventilationParamsAdjustable & !patientConnected & !PSVModeSelected) U OutOfServiceWarning ))

output_LTL: G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning) & G(initFail -> ( (!ventilating & !saveLog & !ventilationParamsAdjustable & !patientConnected & !PSVModeSelected) U OutOfServiceWarning ))

explanation:
We translate the requirement into two parts. First: 'In case of a (initialization) failure the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service.' This means: wherever initFail is true, OutOfServiceWarning must eventually be true. That is, globally: G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning). Second: 'any other operations shall be not allowed' while the system is out-of-service. That is, from the time of initialization failure until the warning is issued, all other operations (other than OutOfServiceWarning and initFail) must not happen.
We define O as the disjunction of all atomic propositions except 'initFail' and 'OutOfServiceWarning'.
The full requirement: G(initFail -> F OutOfServiceWarning) & G((initFail & !OutOfServiceWarning) -> !(O)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Warn user that the system is out-of-service
If initialization fails, eventually the user is warned
Other operations are not allowed until warning is given (all except initFail, OutOfServiceWarning)

explanation: The phrase 'Request r' maps to atomic proposition r, and 'grant g' maps to g. 'Every' implies a global (G) operator, and 'eventually' is represented by F. 'Followed by' expresses a temporal implication, so the final formula is G(r -> F g). If the atomic propositions 'r' and 'g' are not in the permitted list, we may let 'patientBreathingRequest' stand for 'r' (request) and 'ventilating' stand for 'g' (grant/start). So, this maps to: Globally, whenever a patientBreathingRequest happens, eventually ventilating happens: G(patientBreathingRequest -> F ventilating).

explanation:
'Globally if a holds then c is true until b.'
However, using only valid atomic propositions, let's map 'a', 'b', 'c' to valid names. Since you did not provide a specific mapping, we cannot randomly assign atomic props. To complete the response, I will use the following:
Let a = 'powerFailure', b = 'patientSafe', c = 'highPriorityAlarm'.

Then, the sentence is: Globally, if 'powerFailure' holds, then 'highPriorityAlarm' is true until 'patientSafe'.

So the LTL formula is: G(powerFailure -> (highPriorityAlarm U patientSafe))

explanation_dict.natural_language: c is true until b ('highPriorityAlarm' U 'patientSafe')
if a then c U b

explanation:
For 'Every request r is eventually followed by a grant g.', let's pick valid atomic propositions for r and g. For example:
r = 'patientBreathTrigger'
g = 'ventilating'

Then, every patient Breath Trigger is eventually followed by ventilating. The corresponding formula:
G(patientBreathTrigger -> F ventilating)

We interpret the requirement as: If there is an initialization failure (initFail), then (a) the system must eventually warn the user with OutOfServiceWarning, and (b) all other operations become disallowed (i.e., none of the user operations may occur from that point forward). Here, 'other operations' are understood as all atomic propositions that might represent an action, adjustment, or command that the user (operator or system) could perform except for the failure itself and the failure handling (the warning). 

To express 'any other operation is not allowed', we select all atomic propositions that represent system/user actions. From your list, we exclude 'initFail' (the trigger) and 'OutOfServiceWarning' (the required warning). The exact list for 'user/system actions' can be lengthy, so, constructing the negative expression, we conjunct all of these propositions under a big negation. So whenever 'initFail' happens, 'OutOfServiceWarning' must eventually occur (F OutOfServiceWarning), and globally, from now on, for each operation Op_i, !Op_i holds. To express this succinctly in LTL, we use G(initFail -> (F OutOfServiceWarning & G(!(Op1) & !(Op2) & ... & !(OpN)))).

Here is the translation step-by-step:

explanation_dict.natural_language: user is warned system is out-of-service
all other operations (listed) are never allowed after failure
if initialization failure then eventually warn and forbid other operations
globally enforce failure response and operation lockout

explanation: All atomic propositions except 'initFail' and 'OutOfServiceWarning' represent operations not permitted during initialization failure. Thus, the LTL formula asserts: globally, if 'initFail' is true, then 'OutOfServiceWarning' is true, AND all other operations are false. The conjunction enumerates all atomic propositions except 'initFail' and 'OutOfServiceWarning'.

explanation_dict.natural_language: User is warned system is out of service
Any other operation is not allowed
explanation_dict.LTL: conjunction of negated atomic propositions (all except initFail and OutOfServiceWarning)

explanation_dict.natural_language: If initialization failure occurs, then OutOfServiceWarning is true and all other operations are false (globally)

explanation:
The phrase 'Globally if a holds then c is true until b.' must map to valid atomic propositions. Suppose we assign:
- a = initFail (initialization failure)
- c = OutOfServiceWarning (user warning)
- b = initDone (initialization completed)
This gives: 'Whenever initialization fails, the OutOfServiceWarning must continue to hold until initialization is done.' The LTL formula is G(initFail -> (OutOfServiceWarning U initDone)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: c is true until b (OutOfServiceWarning until initDone)
if a holds then c is true until b
